What does the KEY in a pictograph tell you? <svg width="660" height="150" viewBox="0 0 220 52" style="display:block;margin:6px auto;border-radius:6px;background:#f9f9f9"><rect x="4" y="6" width="212" height="18" fill="#e8f4ff" rx="4"/><text x="8" y="19" font-size="9" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Key: 📖 = 2 books</text><text x="8" y="40" font-size="7" fill="#666">Always check the key before counting!</text></svg>
e: The key tells you how much each picture symbol is worth.

t: What is the first step when reading a pictograph? <svg width="660" height="150" viewBox="0 0 220 52" style="display:block;margin:6px auto;border-radius:6px;background:#f9f9f9"><rect x="4" y="6" width="212" height="18" fill="#e8f4ff" rx="4"/><text x="8" y="19" font-size="9" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Key: 📖 = 2 books</text><text x="8" y="40" font-size="7" fill="#666">Always check the key before counting!</text></svg>
e: Always check the key so you know what each picture is worth.

t: In a pictograph where 1 picture = 5, Liam has 2 pictures. How many? <svg width="560" height="144" viewBox="0 0 280 72" style="display:block;margin:6px auto;border-radius:6px;background:#f9f9f9"><rect x="4" y="2" width="272" height="15" fill="#e8f4ff" rx="3"/><text x="8" y="13" font-size="8" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Key: ⭐ = 5</text><line x1="4" y1="17" x2="276" y2="17" stroke="#ddd" stroke-width="1"/><text x="8" y="43" font-size="8" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Liam</text><text x="65" y="47" font-size="16">⭐⭐</text><line x1="4" y1="62" x2="276" y2="62" stroke="#ddd" stroke-width="1"/></svg>

In a pictograph, 1 picture = 3. There are 4 pictures. What is the total? <svg width="560" height="144" viewBox="0 0 280 72" style="display:block;margin:6px auto;border-radius:6px;background:#f9f9f9"><rect x="4" y="2" width="272" height="15" fill="#e8f4ff" rx="3"/><text x="8" y="13" font-size="8" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Key: ⭐ = 3</text><line x1="4" y1="17" x2="276" y2="17" stroke="#ddd" stroke-width="1"/><text x="8" y="43" font-size="8" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Row</text><text x="65" y="47" font-size="16">⭐⭐⭐⭐</text><line x1="4" y1="62" x2="276" y2="62" stroke="#ddd" stroke-width="1"/></svg>

How many more apples? <svg width="560" height="192" viewBox="0 0 280 96" style="display:block;margin:6px auto;border-radius:6px;background:#f9f9f9"><rect x="4" y="2" width="272" height="15" fill="#e8f4ff" rx="3"/><text x="8" y="13" font-size="8" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Key: 🍎 = 2</text><line x1="4" y1="17" x2="276" y2="17" stroke="#ddd" stroke-width="1"/><text x="8" y="38" font-size="8" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Apples</text><text x="65" y="42" font-size="16">🍎🍎🍎🍎🍎</text><line x1="4" y1="56" x2="276" y2="56" stroke="#eee" stroke-width="1"/><text x="8" y="76" font-size="8" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Oranges</text><text x="65" y="80" font-size="16">🍎🍎🍎</text><line x1="4" y1="88" x2="276" y2="88" stroke="#ddd" stroke-width="1"/></svg>

Why do pictographs use pictures instead of numbers? <svg width="660" height="150" viewBox="0 0 220 52" style="display:block;margin:6px auto;border-radius:6px;background:#f9f9f9"><rect x="4" y="6" width="212" height="18" fill="#e8f4ff" rx="4"/><text x="8" y="19" font-size="9" fill="#333" font-weight="bold">Key: 📖 = 2 books</text><text x="8" y="40" font-size="7" fill="#666">Always check the key before counting!</text></svg>
e: Pictures make data easy to see and compare at a glance.
